stories.headline: Global oil demand plummets by most since pandemic, says IEA
stories.summary: Demand drops 3.4% in March due to soaring prices, supply shortages and collapse of Middle East air travel

stories.headline: Global oil demand to plunge amid disruptions caused by war on Iran: IEA
stories.summary: The IEA's oil 'demand destruction' report comes after its chief said unnamed countries are hoarding stocks.

stories.headline: Russia’s Lavrov visits China as US pressures Iran with Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: China and Russia have been vocal critics of the US-Israel war on Iran, an ally of both world powers.

stories.headline: IEA warns of historic oil supply shock as Iran war chokes global markets
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned of an unprecedented energy shock, with global oil production plunging by over 10 million barrels per day in March, as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran fail.

stories.headline: Trump refuses to apologise after clash with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
stories.summary: Despite widespread criticism after the US president lambasted the US-born pontiff as being "weak on crime," Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni's rare rebuke, Trump said no to an apology to the head of the Catholic Church while reiterating his claims.

stories.headline: How much will US Hormuz blockade hurt Iran, and does Tehran have an escape?
stories.summary: Iran earned $5bn in oil exports in past month while it shut the strait for most other ships. That revenue is at risk.

stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said a European Union €90 billion ($106 billion) loan for Ukraine must be disbursed rapidly after an electoral defeat for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban eliminated a major roadblock to the funding.

stories.summary: Failure to repair the protective structure around the nuclear site could unleash 'highly radioactive dust' that 'does not recognise borders', experts warn.

stories.headline: UK Pays Highest Yield for 10-Year Gilt Sale Since 2008
stories.summary: The UK sold 10-year bonds at the highest yield since the global financial crisis, drawing record numbers of buyers keen to lock in returns that may fall back if the Middle East war comes to an end.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
stories.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy pitches new joint security system to European allies

stories.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing next week for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, the panel’s top Republican said Tuesday.

stories.headline: Dollar and VIX Are Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
stories.summary: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

stories.headline: US, Iran Weigh More Talks; United Pitches Tie-Up with American Airlines | Bloomberg Brief 4/14/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures rise and oil slips on optimism as the US and Iran consider another round of peace talks. United CEO Scott Kirby is said to have pitched a possible tie-up with American Airlines. Bank earnings continue with JPMorgan, Wells Fargo and Citi. stories.headline: Golden Goose Starts Bond Sale Linked to PE Firm Buyout
stories.summary: Golden Goose Group SpA has started marketing a €880 million ($1.04 billion) bond sale to fund its acquisition by Chinese private equity firm HSG, a test of investor sentiment given struggles in the luxury sector and concerns around the Iran war.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Traders Post Highest-Ever Quarterly Revenue
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase pulled in $11.6 billion in trading revenue in the first quarter, up 20% from a year earlier. Record stock-trading results boosted the total past the firm’s previous record by almost $2 billion. Dani Burger reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

stories.headline: Lucid Names CEO as Saudi PIF, Uber Commit Another $750 Million
stories.summary: Lucid Group Inc. named a new chief executive officer and announced $750 million of fresh investment from its two biggest backers as the electric-car maker looks to shore up operations at a crucial juncture.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea

stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Nasdaq 100 Eyes Best Run in Years on Peace Push: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: The Nasdaq 100 looked set to notch its longest streak of gains since 2021 as optimism that the US and Iran are considering another round of peace talks pushed oil lower and lifted stocks globally.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.
